title: Hedyotis wynaadensis
intro: Hedyotis wynaadensis, commonly known as Wynaad nutweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis wynaadensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hedyotis wynaadensis is often used to prepare herbal formulations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin diseases.

Additionally, Hedyotis wynaadensis has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Its medicinal uses are largely based on its bioactive compounds and phytochemical properties.
